Politics.headline: Kamala Harris says she might run for president in 2028: "I'm thinking about it"
Politics.detail: After chants of “run again!” filled the room, former Vice President Kamala Harris told African American activists that she's actively considering another presidential bid.

Politics.headline: Brazil announces US partnership to intercept weapons, drug trafficking
Politics.detail: Brazil and the U.S. have announced a partnership aimed at intercepting weapons and drug trafficking.

Politics.headline: Mamdani hits 100 days as mayor of New York City
Politics.detail: Democrat Mamdani marks 100 days as mayor of New York City, and it remains to be seen whether he'll be able to leverage his fame into achieving the progressive policy proposals that propelled him to office.

Politics.headline: U.S. and Iran prepare for ceasefire talks as Netanyahu authorizes negotiations with Lebanon
Politics.detail: The U.S. and Iran are preparing for ceasefire talks as Netanyahu authorizes negotiations with Lebanon.

Politics.headline: Democrats shut down talk of impeaching Trump this year
Politics.detail: Democrats have shut down any talk of impeaching Trump this year.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Inflation Surges to 3.3% in March
Finance & Economy.detail: The annual inflation rate in the US jumped to 3.3% in March, the highest since May 2024, primarily driven by higher energy costs linked to the war with Iran; consumer prices rose 0.9% on the month, the largest increase since June 2022.

Finance & Economy.headline: Shipping in Strait of Hormuz at a Standstill Despite US-Iran Ceasefire
Finance & Economy.detail: Despite the announced ceasefire, shipping in the Strait of Hormuz remains at a standstill, causing continued concern for global trade and energy supplies.

Finance & Economy.headline: US consumer sentiment dives to a record low in April amid Iran war.
Finance & Economy.detail: Consumer sentiment in the United States has plummeted to a record low in April, reflecting the economic uncertainty and anxiety fueled by the ongoing war with Iran.

Finance & Economy.headline: Bank of Canada, major lenders meet on Anthropic AI cyber risk
Finance & Economy.detail: Canadian regulators and major lenders are convening to address the escalating cyber risks associated with increasingly powerful AI models like Anthropic, which could lead to a new wave of sophisticated cyber attacks.

Finance & Economy.headline: Energy prices may take 'months' to normalise, despite ceasefire: Analysts
Finance & Economy.detail: Analysts predict that it may take months for energy prices to stabilize, even with the US-Iran ceasefire, emphasizing the need for a predictable and stable flow of cargo through the Strait of Hormuz.

Technology.headline: Microsoft Warns of Hackers Supercharging Cyberattacks With AI
Technology.detail: AI is becoming a force multiplier for cybercriminals, helping threat actors automate phishing, malware development, and reconnaissance while accelerating their attacks.

Technology.headline: Logitech Takes Aim at Bulky Boardroom Gear With AI Cameras
Technology.detail: Logitech's Rally AI cameras combine AI-driven framing, flexible mounting, and room analytics in a compact alternative to traditional conferencing hardware.

Technology.headline: Samsung Hikes Some Galaxy Z Fold 7 Prices, Now $80 More for 1TB Version
Technology.detail: The price of the 1TB version of the Samsung Galaxy Z Fold 7 has increased.

Technology.headline: US summons bank bosses over cyber risks from Anthropic's latest AI model
Technology.detail: Bank bosses are being summoned to discuss cyber risks associated with Anthropic's new AI model.

Technology.headline: Ultrasonic wristband used to track hand movements like never before
Technology.detail: MIT researchers have developed an ultrasound wristband that uses AI to translate wrist movements into precise, real-time robotic actions, proving to be an effective method for teaching robots to move like humans.

Science.headline: Artemis II splashdown: Astronauts return to Earth after lunar mission
Science.detail: The Artemis II crew landed in the Pacific Ocean under parachutes after a high-speed re-entry, completing a landmark mission that carried humans around the Moon and back for the first time in over 50 years. The crew successfully completed a parachute landing on Friday and will undergo medical checks before returning to NASA's Johnson Space Center in Houston.

Science.headline: Artemis II's awe reminds us of science's fragility and importance
Science.detail: From space exploration to medical breakthroughs, humanity's successes have been the product of curiosity, ingenuity, scientists' perseverance and governments willing to fund them. The record-setting flyby is a reminder of what could happen when humans come together to realize a lofty goal.

Science.headline: Scientists Found a Common Brain 'Fingerprint' Across 5 Psychedelics
Science.detail: Researchers have identified a common brain fingerprint across five different psychedelic substances. This discovery could provide insights into how these drugs affect the brain and potentially lead to new treatments for mental health conditions.

Science.headline: Space Is Dead Silent – But There Is a Way to 'Hear' a Black Hole
Science.detail: Even though space is a vacuum and sound waves can't travel, scientists have found a way to 'hear' a black hole. By analyzing the ripples in spacetime caused by black hole mergers, they can translate these gravitational waves into audible sounds, providing a new way to study these mysterious objects.

Science.headline: Common Vitamin May Reduce Buildup of Alzheimer's Proteins, Study Finds
Science.detail: A new study suggests that a common vitamin may help reduce the buildup of proteins associated with Alzheimer's disease. Researchers found that this vitamin can promote the clearance of amyloid plaques, potentially offering a new avenue for preventing or treating the disease.

Health.headline: Replimune's Drug for Advanced Skin Cancer Fails to Win FDA Nod Again
Health.detail: The FDA has declined to approve Replimune's drug for advanced skin cancer for the second time, marking a setback in the treatment's development.

Health.headline: Acute Severe Ulcerative Colitis: 10 Risks in Older Adults
Health.detail: Older adults with acute severe ulcerative colitis face higher mortality rates, highlighting the urgent need for targeted strategies to mitigate risks and facilitate timely treatment decisions.

Health.headline: Massachusetts man speaks again after pioneering larynx transplant
Health.detail: Surgeons in Massachusetts have successfully performed a larynx transplant on a man whose cancerous voice box was removed, making him only the third patient in the U.S. to receive such a transplant and the first with active cancer.

Health.headline: Alzheimer's isn't just one problem—it's a tangled mix of biology, aging, and overall health
Health.detail: Scientists are shifting towards multifaceted approaches to tackle Alzheimer's, acknowledging it as a complex interplay of biological factors, aging, and overall health, which is why single-target drugs have shown limited success.

Health.headline: New ACIP Charter, Published Thursday, Revises Purpose Of Panel, Who's On It
Health.detail: A revised charter for the Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP) has been published, outlining changes to the panel's purpose and membership.

Sports.headline: Rory McIlroy pulls away with birdie binge and sets Masters record with 6-shot lead
Sports.detail: Rory McIlroy birdied six of the last seven holes at the Masters, achieving a tournament-best 65 and turning the event into a potential victory lap. He now holds a significant lead.

Sports.headline: IPL 2026: Sooryavanshi, Jurel's Fiery Knocks Hand Unbeaten RR Win Over RCB
Sports.detail: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Dhruv Jurel's impressive batting performances led Rajasthan Royals to a victory over Royal Challengers Bengaluru in the IPL. Sooryavanshi's innings helped RR achieve the highest powerplay score in the league this season.

Sports.headline: Ichiro Suzuki's statue unveiling has a mishap as bat snaps during ceremony
Sports.detail: During the unveiling of Ichiro Suzuki's statue, an unexpected incident occurred when the bat broke. The ceremony still proceeded despite the mishap.

Sports.headline: Premier League Clinches Fifth UEFA Champions League Spot After Arsenal Victory
Sports.detail: The Premier League has secured a fifth spot in the UEFA Champions League following a victory by Arsenal. This is a significant achievement for the league.

Sports.headline: Kirk Cousins reveals how a FaceTime call with Tom Brady sealed his decision to join the Raiders
Sports.detail: Kirk Cousins shared that a FaceTime conversation with Tom Brady played a crucial role in his decision to join the Las Vegas Raiders. The call apparently provided the final push needed for Cousins to sign with the team.

Entertainment.headline: HYBE's Latin boy band Santos Bravos looks to BTS, Bad Bunny with K-pop playbook
Entertainment.detail: K-pop powerhouse HYBE's first Latin boy band Santos Bravos landed in Korea and member Gabi admitted that there's a lot of weight to carry as the first Latin pop group coming from HYBE. The band is looking to emulate the success of BTS and Bad Bunny by utilizing the K-pop production system.

Entertainment.headline: Jury reaches no verdict on first day deliberating at Live Nation ticket monopoly trial
Entertainment.detail: A jury has finished its first day of deliberations without reaching a verdict in a civil case pitting 34 states against the concert giant Live Nation Entertainment.

Entertainment.headline: Ranbir Kapoor talks about his role in Ramayana
Entertainment.detail: Ranbir Kapoor says that while he doesn't love the promotion side of his job, he's happy to do it for “Ramayana,” an ambitious new two-part film epic aiming to share the sacred Hindu stories with audiences around the world.

Entertainment.headline: NASA drops Artemis II moon mission playlist with astronauts’ wake-up songs
Entertainment.detail: NASA released the Artemis II moon mission playlist, featuring the songs that will wake up the astronauts during their historic mission.

Entertainment.headline: Tom Holland Confirms Spider-Man: Brand New Day Reshoots
Entertainment.detail: Tom Holland has confirmed that reshoots are taking place for Spider-Man: Brand New Day.

Crime & Justice.headline: Five charged with murder in fatal US fireworks warehouse explosion
Crime & Justice.detail: Five people have been charged with murder in connection to a deadly Northern California explosion at a fireworks warehouse that killed seven people. The charges stem from a grand jury indictment that found the individuals responsible for the explosion that injured two others.

Crime & Justice.headline: Police arrest a suspect in Molotov attack at OpenAI CEO's home
Crime & Justice.detail: A 20-year-old man was arrested for allegedly throwing a Molotov cocktail at OpenAI CEO Sam Altman's San Francisco home and then making threats at the company's headquarters. The incident occurred early Friday morning, with the suspect setting an exterior gate on fire before fleeing.

Crime & Justice.headline: Trump shares video of brutal Florida killing allegedly by Haitian immigrant
Crime & Justice.detail: Donald Trump shared a video of a brutal killing in Florida, allegedly committed by a Haitian immigrant, sparking controversy. The victim was a convenience store clerk, and the suspect, who had Temporary Protective Status, has confessed to the crime.

Crime & Justice.headline: DOJ Drops 23,000 Cases To Nab a Kid in a Bunny Hat – Update for April 11, 2026
Crime & Justice.detail: The Department of Justice (DOJ) reportedly closed over 23,000 criminal cases, including investigations into terrorism and white-collar crime, to shift resources to immigration cases. This occurred in the first six months of President Trump's administration, with the bulk of cases being closed without prosecution.

Crime & Justice.headline: Appeal dismissed in impaired driving case involving THC, death of 9-year-old
Crime & Justice.detail: The appeal against a woman who was driving when 9-year-old Baeleigh Maurice died in 2021 has been dismissed after a series of delays. The case involved THC impairment.

Education.headline: Chicago teachers want no school on May Day, testing the city's mayor and school leaders
Education.detail: A political fight has emerged in Chicago over whether public schools will be in session on May Day.

Education.headline: University of Michigan shifts funding from DEI into its free tuition program, Go Blue Guarantee
Education.detail: The University of Michigan is reallocating funds from Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) initiatives to support its Go Blue Guarantee, a program providing free tuition to eligible students.

Education.headline: Government launches new research funding to strengthen school food programs across Canada
Education.detail: The Canadian government has announced new research funding aimed at bolstering school food programs throughout the country.

Education.headline: AUArts Announces New Bachelor of Creative Industries Degree Linking Art, Craft, Design, Industry and Technology
Education.detail: Alberta University of the Arts (AUArts) has announced a new Bachelor of Creative Industries degree that integrates art, craft, design, industry, and technology.

Education.headline: Clearview Public Schools Names Esther Nooijen as 2026 Edwin Parr Teacher Award Nominee
Education.detail: Clearview Public Schools has announced Esther Nooijen as their nominee for the 2026 Edwin Parr Teacher Award.

Lifestyle.headline: New exhibition opens to mark 200 years of Harrogate's first hospital
Lifestyle.detail: A new exhibition has opened in Harrogate, UK, to commemorate the 200th anniversary of the town's first hospital.

Lifestyle.headline: Redefining the game with 'phases'
Lifestyle.detail: Thembelihle Lee-Anne Nkonyane, also known as Expo, is releasing her debut album 'Phases', marking her transition from DJ to producer in the Deep House scene. The album is a collective effort, with one track, 'Eclipse', generating anticipation due to a high-profile collaboration.

Lifestyle.headline: 'Butter Birkin': popcorn plastic It bag in demand by Devil Wears Prada fans
Lifestyle.detail: A popcorn plastic bag is gaining popularity among fans of The Devil Wears Prada. This unique handbag has been dubbed the 'Butter Birkin'.

Lifestyle.headline: Social media use in Türkiye exceeds global average, raising addiction concerns
Lifestyle.detail: Experts in Türkiye are highlighting the country's high social media usage and associated risks, noting that people are spending extended periods online as a way to cope with emotional challenges, which is affecting their real-life interactions and family life. A personal story illustrates how support can aid in overcoming dependency.

Lifestyle.headline: 'This is as important as your teeth': are you skipping this key part of mouth hygiene?
Lifestyle.detail: Dentists are saying that cleaning your tongue is as important as cleaning your teeth and that everyone needs to be educated about it.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL — Some judicial restraint
Opinion/Editorial.detail: With fuel prices in the country staying in the stratosphere despite the ceasefire in the Middle East, there was a positive development regarding the accountability of those in high office.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Fragile
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Talks between the US and Iran should be underway today in Islamabad, however, crude oil prices softened after Donald Trump announced a ceasefire in the war but prices quickly bounced up again after the world realized what a fragile arrangement this is.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: A proper end to the Race
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The Revitalizing the Automotive Industry for Competitiveness Enhancement (Race) program was effectively canceled when President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. vetoed its proposed P250-million budget for 2026 back in January, and the Department of Trade and Industry (DTI) will no longer pursue it, and will put its efforts into rolling out the Electric Vehicle Incentive Strategy (Evis).

Opinion/Editorial.headline: The Middle East war has brought a silver lining for China's economy
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The US and Israeli attacks on Iran stand to benefit Chinese President Xi Jinping by helping fend off the specter of deflation, as the conflict might do some of the Chinese central bank's work for it.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: U.S. missile burn rate in Iran leaves the Pacific cupboard bare
Opinion/Editorial.detail: The U.S. missile burn rate in Iran leaves the Pacific cupboard bare.

World.headline: Artemis II astronauts splash down on Earth after lunar mission
World.detail: The Artemis II crew landed in the Pacific Ocean under parachutes after a high-speed re-entry that tested its heat shield, marking the end of a historic NASA moon mission.

World.headline: New tensions emerge before US-Iran war ceasefire talks in Pakistan
World.detail: Ahead of planned ceasefire talks between the US and Iran in Pakistan, new tensions have surfaced, potentially complicating the negotiation process.

World.headline: Russia's Putin declares a ceasefire in Ukraine for Orthodox Easter
World.detail: Putin has announced a ceasefire in Ukraine to observe Orthodox Easter, though the duration and impact remain to be seen.

World.headline: Prince Harry sued for defamation by charity he co-founded
World.detail: Prince Harry is facing a defamation lawsuit from Sentebale, the charity he co-founded, following a dispute with the organization's chair after his resignation as patron.

World.headline: Multiple people face charges, including murder, in California fireworks blast
World.detail: Following a fireworks explosion in California, several individuals are facing charges, including murder, related to the incident.

Zimbabwe.headline: Govt policies bearing fruit: President
Zimbabwe.detail: President Mnangagwa says government policies are yielding positive results.

Zimbabwe.headline: Lands, Agriculture Ministry split into two
Zimbabwe.detail: The Lands and Agriculture Ministry has been divided into two separate ministries.

Zimbabwe.headline: UN delegation hails Zim's Security Council bid
Zimbabwe.detail: A United Nations delegation has expressed support for Zimbabwe's bid for a seat on the Security Council.

Zimbabwe.headline: 'Govt pursues heritage‑driven nation building'
Zimbabwe.detail: The government is actively promoting nation-building through heritage-focused initiatives.

Zimbabwe.headline: President splits agriculture ministry to boost eficiency
Zimbabwe.detail: The President has divided the agriculture ministry to enhance efficiency.
